nonclaims: FARA has no value beyond directness and constraint enforcement
FARA can never be reasoning-specific under another preregistered test
the seven criteria are necessary or sufficient for reasoning generally
preserved_failures: FARA uniqueness is not supported at the registered scope.
Perfect positive/contrast separation is based on project-authored semantic licensing and a finite synthetic corpus.
The two disputed cases remain borderline.
Candidate ablation, reconstruction, complete cost accounting, and claim-impact closure remain unexecuted.

reasoning.conclusion: FARA makes the seven roles directly addressable and enforces a stricter schema, but the registered discriminator is not unique to FARA because the same criterion vector is available on the candidate-neutral source and survives exact GREL recovery.
